chunk_summary: Guidance on carbohydrate quality in children emphasizes minimally processed staples, free sugar limits, and appropriate fibre intake, with specific recommendations for beverages and sweeteners.
learning_objectives: Understand the role of carbohydrate quality in pediatric nutrition, including staple food choices.
Learn WHO and AAP guidelines on free sugar and fruit juice limits for children.
Recognize the importance of gradual fibre introduction and managing intolerance symptoms.
Counsel parents on avoiding sugary beverages and non-sugar sweeteners.
key_facts: Carbohydrates should supply roughly 50% of total energy in children.
WHO recommends free sugars below 10% of energy, with additional benefits below 5%.
AAP advises no fruit juice under 12 months, 120 mL/day for 1–3 years, and 120–180 mL/day for 4–6 years.
Energy drinks should be avoided entirely in children and adolescents.
Non-sugar sweeteners are not recommended for routine use in children per WHO 2023 guidelines.
Fibre should be increased gradually with water to prevent constipation, using whole grains, fruits, and vegetables.
reference_quote: The WHO sugars guideline recommends keeping free sugars below 10% of energy, with additional benefit below 5%.
document_key_topics: Free sugar and fruit juice guidelines
Beverage and sweetener recommendations for children
